# TaskFlow — Product Requirements Document (1-page, retrofit)
*Reference example · generated by interview-style prompting, grounded in the code*

## Problem
Small teams lose track of who is doing what and by when. Chat threads and spreadsheets don't show status at a glance, and heavier project tools are overkill. Teams need a shared, lightweight board.

## Target users
Members of a small team (5–15 people) who create and track their own tasks. No admin or PM role required — anyone can see and use the board.

## Goals
- See all team work, grouped by status, in one place.
- Create and assign tasks in seconds.
- Move work across a simple lifecycle without training.

## Non-goals (explicitly out of scope)
Authentication/SSO, real-time multi-user sync, comments, attachments, and reporting. Notifications are handled by a separate service (see M4); analytics is future work.

## Requirements (with acceptance criteria)
1. **Create a task** — title required; assignee and due date optional; defaults to *To-Do*. *AC: a blank title is rejected; the new task appears immediately.*
2. **Move a task** — status is one of *To-Do · In Progress · Done*; any transition allowed. *AC: status persists across a restart.*
3. **View the board** — three columns; each card shows title, assignee, due date. *AC: all tasks visible, grouped by status.*
4. **Filter** — by assignee or status; clearing restores all. *AC: filter narrows the visible set correctly.*

## Success metrics
- A new user creates their first task in **< 30 seconds**, unaided.
- **≥ 90%** of a pilot team's active work is represented on the board within one week.
- Board loads and reflects a change in **< 1 second** on the deployed app.

## Constraints
Runs on Replit's free tier (web + Replit DB); no local install for users; mobile companion via Expo. Must stay a small, readable codebase.

---
*Notice: every requirement maps to real behavior in the TaskFlow code and to a testable acceptance criterion. That traceability is what makes this a PRD and not a wish-list.*
